costs: Requires increased or reallocated public spending within an already low overall education-spending envelope
Administrative cost of designing and monitoring targeted funding formulas
Evaluation infrastructure costs given documented lack of a shared trusted measurement baseline
content.equity_impact: Likely to produce incremental, bounded equity gains for students remaining in comprehensive schools, but does not address the between-school segregation mechanism itself, which is the dominant driver of Hungary's outlier PISA-measured inequality; best framed as a floor-raising measure rather than a structural fix.
expected_benefits: Politically low-friction path since it does not touch the constitutionally protected choice architecture or the entrenched track structure
Directly compensates the documented funding/peer-effect loss mechanism in feeder schools
Builds on existing legal and administrative infrastructure (2023 reform) rather than requiring new legislation
content.goal: Pursue equity and achievement gains through targeted compensatory investment and quality-levelling measures while leaving the 10-12 selection age and track structure untouched.

implementation_steps.action: Design a targeted supplementary funding stream for schools with high shares of disadvantaged students and high departure rates to selective tracks (TEIP-style)
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Education / Ministry of Finance

implementation_steps.action: Revise curriculum guidance for comprehensive-track schools to improve clarity and reduce reliance on informal test-prep culture
Implement retention-reduction measures (early intervention, tutoring support) targeted at grades most affected by peer-composition dilution
Commission independent evaluation of the 2023 teacher-career reform and incentive funding for equity impact, and expand if positive

mechanisms: Capitation funding causes feeder schools to lose both student funding and positive peer effects when high-performers depart for selective tracks, with no documented compensatory mechanism, making targeted counter-funding a direct lever against this specific loss (F-live-education_finance-05).

Only 12.1% of disadvantaged students reach a good level in at least one basic skill versus 16.3% EU average, indicating substantial room for targeted skill-support investment independent of tracking structure (F-live-education_finance-07, F-live-implementation_planning-03).

Hungary's overall low spending share (4.1%, rank 41/42) and low instructional time (rank 32/34) make internal reallocation toward disadvantaged/feeder schools more consequential than in higher-spending systems (F-live-education_finance-06).

The 2023 teacher-career reform (Act LII) and subject-teacher incentive funding already exist as a non-structural quality lever but remain unevaluated for equity impact, indicating an existing policy channel to strengthen and evaluate rather than invent from scratch (F-live-portuguese_reform-07).
risks: Structural segregation mechanisms (tracking, free choice, church-school growth) remain fully intact, so compensatory funding may only partially offset ongoing sorting rather than reverse it (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07)
Disputed statistics baseline between government and civil society could undermine credible evaluation of whether targeted funding is working (F-live-implementation_planning-09)

Within-school sorting (classroom-level SES sorting) would likely persist and could absorb any gains from school-level funding without also addressing classroom composition (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10)
content.title: Non-structural compensatory equity package (targeted funding, curriculum clarity, retention reduction) with selection age unchanged
